fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to district of columbia, pharmacy technician compact district of columbia, district of columbia pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: District of Columbia Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)
board.mailing_address: DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection, 1100 4th Street SW, Suite 500W, Washington, DC 20024

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years)
quick_facts.license_duration: Up to 2 Years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years)

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Create an Account on the DLCP Online Services Portal
steps.description: Visit https://apply.dc.gov/ and register for a new user account if you do not already have one. You will need to provide a valid email address and create a password.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Log in to your DLCP Online Services Portal account. Navigate to 'Occupational and Professional Licensing' and select 'Apply for a New License' or similar, then choose 'Pharmacy Technician' from the list of professions.

steps.title: Complete the Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form accurately. This will include personal information, educational background, and any past work history.

steps.title: Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, which typically include proof of high school diploma/GED, official transcripts from an approved pharmacy technician program (if not nationally certified), proof of passing a national certification exam (PTCB or NHA), and a completed Criminal Background Check Request Form.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fees
steps.description: Follow the prompts within the portal to pay the required initial application and license fees, which total $200.00 for new pharmacy technician licenses.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Review your application thoroughly before submitting. After submission, monitor your application status through the DLCP Online Services Portal for any updates or requests for additional information from the Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: District of Columbia offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the District of Columbia Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in District of Columbia by submitting a new application to the District of Columbia Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any District of Columbia-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in District of Columbia by submitting a new application to the District of Columbia Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any District of Columbia-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in District of Columbia
hero.dek: Apply online through the DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection (DLCP) Online Services Portal (apply.dc.gov). Create an account, select 'Pharmacy Technician' under new applications, and submit your required documents and payment.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the DLCP Online Services Portal, click 'Register for an Account' to create a new user profile with a valid email address and password, then log in to start an application.
